scoping_rule: The OpenSpec workflow is a shared task-environment property, never a condition or arm. Both arms work inside the same OpenSpec-initialized workspace; the causal variable remains executable-feedback availability. Runs under this profile form their own compatibility boundary and are never pooled with base-profile runs.

workflow_profile.telemetry_policy: Every CLI invocation sets OPENSPEC_TELEMETRY=0, DO_NOT_TRACK=1, NO_COLOR=1, FORCE_COLOR=0, CI=1 with non-TTY stdio. The opt-out mechanism is pinned by characterization tests; drift requires re-sealing this profile.

archive_step.failure_detection: Output text ('Aborted. No files were changed.') and the still-present change directory determine failure; the pinned CLI exits 0 on abort, so exit codes are never trusted alone.
archive_step.archive_rename: The dated archive directory (YYYY-MM-DD-<change>) is renamed to the plain change name immediately after a successful archive so workspace state stays replay-deterministic across days.
archive_step.failed_archive_policy: A failed archive step leaves the spec-of-record unchanged, is recorded on the run bundle, and the run continues; archive failure is agent outcome (a malformed or missing delta), not harness failure.
archive_step.regression_surface_rule: Archive applies MODIFIED-replace semantics: a MODIFIED requirement block wholesale replaces the existing block, so omitted previously-established scenarios are silently dropped from the spec-of-record. This workflow-native prose-regression surface is present identically in both arms.

workflow_profile.spec_of_record_mutation_rule: Agent FILE replacements into openspec/specs/ are refused as read-only; the harness-run archive step is the only mutator of the spec-of-record. Agent-authored change deltas under openspec/changes/ are the workflow's spec-maintenance surface.
workflow_profile.scenario_parity_rule: At the first checkpoint's start (fresh mounts), both arms' spec-of-record scenario sets must be canonically identical (canonicalizer strips Gherkin keywords, markdown emphasis, bullets, case, and whitespace); a fresh-mount divergence fails the run. From the first agent-maintained checkpoint onward, cross-arm spec-of-record divergence is measured outcome recorded per checkpoint (and in the survival ledger), not a parity violation.

survival_ledger.rule: Each archive step records scenarios dropped, added, and changed in the spec-of-record. The ledger is descriptive context for prose-regression analysis; the protocol primary metric remains the sealed hidden-oracle checkpoint_mean_cumulative_hidden_assertion_pass_rate_v1.

task_package_fields.workflow_changes: Optional map of checkpoint id to the change name the harness archives at that checkpoint's end; checkpoints without an entry run no archive step.
